title: What Does the Datadog CTO's Sale of Company Shares Worth $11.5 Million Mean to Investors?
excerpt: The provider of a cloud-based observability and security platform reported a notable insider sale after its stock achieved an 81% one-year return.
content_html:
Robert Izquierdo, The Motley Fool

Wed, July 22, 2026 at 9:00 PM CDT 5 min read

Alexis Le-Quoc, co-founder and Chief Technology Officer of Datadog, Inc. (NASDAQ:DDOG), sold 43,224 shares of Class A Common Stock on July 20, 2026, for a total value of ~$11.5 million, according to an SEC Form 4 filing.

Transaction summary

Transaction value based on SEC Form 4 weighted average sale price ($265.23); post-transaction value based on July 20, 2026 market close ($263.20).

Key questions

  • What triggered this specific liquidation of equity?
    The transaction was part of a structured divestment process governed by a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted on June 13, 2025. This plan facilitated the automatic exercise of 43,224 options and their immediate sale on the open market, providing the executive with liquidity while maintaining a substantial long-term stake in Datadog.

  • How does the current holding compare to historical equity awards?
    While the sale involved ~43,000 shares, Alexis Le-Quoc continues to hold a significant portfolio of company equity. This includes 509,805 shares held directly and approximately 8.4 million derivative securities, including vested and unvested awards, held both directly and through the Alexis Le-Quoc Revocable Trust.

  • How has the stock performed relative to the transaction price?
    The disposition occurred at a weighted average price of $265.23, while the one-year total return for the stock stood at 81% as of the July 20, 2026 transaction date. Since the trade, the share price settled at $254.79 as of the July 21, 2026 market close.

  • What is the impact on total ownership concentration?
    The transaction resulted in an 8% reduction in direct holdings, leaving the insider with a total beneficial interest of 509,974 shares of Class A Common Stock. This remaining position, excluding derivatives, represents an insider ownership percentage of 0.14% and a market valuation of approximately $130 million based on recent trading levels.

Company Overview

Company Snapshot

  • Datadog provides a comprehensive cloud-based Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) platform that delivers infrastructure monitoring, application performance management, log management, and security surveillance capabilities to enterprise customers globally.

  • The company operates on a subscription-based business model, generating recurring revenue from customers who rely on its integrated monitoring and analytics solutions to maintain real-time visibility across their technology infrastructure.

  • Datadog serves developers, IT operations teams, and business stakeholders across North America and internationally, with particular strength in enterprises requiring end-to-end observability and security monitoring solutions.

This plan facilitated the automatic exercise of 43,224 options and their immediate sale on the open market, providing the executive with liquidity while maintaining a substantial long-term stake in Datadog. How does the current holding compare to historical equity awards?While the sale involved ~43,000 shares, Alexis Le-Quoc continues to hold a significant portfolio of company equity. This includes 509,805 shares held directly and approximately 8.4 million derivative securities, including vested and unvested awards, held both directly and through the Alexis Le-Quoc Revocable Trust. Company Overview Company Snapshot Datadog provides a comprehensive cloud-based Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) platform that delivers infrastructure monitoring, application performance management, log management, and security surveillance capabilities to enterprise customers globally. The company operates on a subscription-based business model, generating recurring revenue from customers who rely on its integrated monitoring and analytics solutions to maintain real-time visibility across their technology infrastructure. Datadog serves developers, IT operations teams, and business stakeholders across North America and internationally, with particular strength in enterprises requiring end-to-end observability and security monitoring solutions.

content_html_zh:
罗伯特·伊斯奎尔多，杂色傻瓜

2026 年 7 月 22 日星期三，CDT 晚上 9:00 阅读时间 5 分钟

根据 SEC Form 4 备案，Datadog, Inc.（纳斯达克股票代码：DDOG）联合创始人兼首席技术官 Alexis Le-Quoc 于 2026 年 7 月 20 日出售了 43,224 股 A 类普通股，总价值约 1150 万美元。

交易概要

交易价值基于 SEC Form 4 加权平均售价（265.23 美元）；交易后价值基于 2026 年 7 月 20 日市场收盘价（263.20 美元）。

关键问题

  • 是什么触发了这种特定的股权清算？该交易是结构性撤资流程的一部分，受 2025 年 6 月 13 日通过的规则 10b5-1 交易计划管辖。 该计划促进了 43,224 份期权的自动行使及其在公开市场上的立即出售，为高管提供了流动性，同时保留了 Datadog 的大量长期股权。
  • 目前的持股与历史股权奖励相比如何？虽然此次出售涉及约 43,000 股股票，但 Alexis Le-Quoc 仍持有大量公司股权。 其中包括直接持有的 509,805 股股票和约 840 万股衍生证券，包括直接持有和通过 Alexis Le-Quoc 可撤销信托持有的既得和未归属奖励。
  • 该股票相对于交易价格的表现如何？此次处置的加权平均价格为 265.23 美元，而截至 2026 年 7 月 20 日交易日，该股票的一年总回报率为 81%。 自交易以来，截至 2026 年 7 月 21 日收盘，股价结算于 254.79 美元。
  • 对总所有权集中度有什么影响？交易导致直接持股减少8%，使内部人总共受益于509,974股A类普通股。 剩余头寸（不包括衍生品）的内部持股比例为 0.14%，根据最近的交易水平，其市场估值约为 1.3 亿美元。

公司概况

公司概况

  • Datadog 提供全面的基于云的软件即服务 (SaaS) 平台，为全球企业客户提供基础设施监控、应用程序性能管理、日志管理和安全监控功能。
  • 该公司采用基于订阅的业务模式运营，从依赖其集成监控和分析解决方案的客户那里产生经常性收入，以保持其技术基础设施的实时可见性。
  • Datadog 为北美和国际各地的开发人员、IT 运营团队和业务利益相关者提供服务，在需要端到端可观察性和安全监控解决方案的企业中尤其具有优势。
